{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miidani Kofun cluster | native_name =箕谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miidani Kofun Group 2go-fun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miidani Kofun-gun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miidani Kofun-gun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|24|1|N|134|45|13|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Yabu, Hyōgo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|1|N|134|45|13|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miidani Kofun cluster'''|箕谷古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s, located in the Yokachō Koyama neighborhood of the city of [[Yabu, Hyōgo]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The group consists of five tumuli, four of which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139097 |title=箕谷古墳群 |trans-title= Miidani Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miidani Kofun cluster is located in the center of the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]], on a hill on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Yagi River, at an elevation of 83 to 93 meters. The group consists of four small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}}-style (circular) burial mounds, all of which have been considerably flattened by erosion, exposing the horizontal stone [[burial chamber]]s. The No.2 tumulus was slightly oval, measuring 12 by 14 meters; the No.3 tumulus was even more oval at 13.5 by 9.5 meters, and had ''[[fukiishi]]''. The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.
From [[grave goods]] recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of [[Sue ware]] pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the [[Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] revealed had a [[Chinese calendar]] dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that [[Ono no Imoko]] departed on the [[Japanese missions to Sui China|Third embassy to Sui China]].
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from [[Yōka Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Front of Miidani Kofun Group.jpg|Panorama Miidani2goufun-20080923.JPG|Tumulus No.2 Miidani Kofun Group 3go-fun.jpg|Tumulus No.3 Miidani Kofun Group 4go-fun.jpg|Tumulus No.4 Miidani Kofun Group 5go-fun.jpg|Tumulus No.5 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Miidani Kofun Group}} *[https://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/midanikohun Yabu Tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/shakaikyoiku/1/4/mi123/index.html Yabu City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The group consists of five tumuli, four of which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139097 |title=箕谷古墳群 |trans-title= Miidani Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miidani Kofun cluster is located in the center of the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]], on a hill on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Yagi River, at an elevation of 83 to 93 meters. The group consists of four small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}}-style (circular) burial mounds, all of which have been considerably flattened by erosion, exposing the horizontal stone [[burial chamber]]s. The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.
From [[grave goods]] recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miidani Kofun cluster | native_name =箕谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miidani Kofun Group 2go-fun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miidani Kofun-gun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miidani Kofun-gun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|24|1|N|134|45|13|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Yabu, Hyōgo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|1|N|134|45|13|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miidani Kofun cluster'''|箕谷古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s, located in the Yokachō Koyama neighborhood of the city of [[Yabu, Hyōgo]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The group consists of five tumuli, four of which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139097 |title=箕谷古墳群 |trans-title= Miidani Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miidani Kofun cluster is located in the center of the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]], on a hill on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Yagi River, at an elevation of 83 to 93 meters. The group consists of four small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}}-style (circular) burial mounds, all of which have been considerably flattened by erosion, exposing the horizontal stone [[burial chamber]]s. The No.2 tumulus was slightly oval, measuring 12 by 14 meters; the No.3 tumulus was even more oval at 13.5 by 9.5 meters, and had ''[[fukiishi]]''. The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.
From [[grave goods]] recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of [[Sue ware]] pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the [[Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] revealed had a [[Chinese calendar]] dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that [[Ono no Imoko]] departed on the [[Japanese missions to Sui China|Third embassy to Sui China]].
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from [[Yōka Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.
From [[grave goods]] recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of [[Sue ware]] pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the [[Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] revealed had a [[Chinese calendar]] dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that [[Ono no Imoko]] departed on the [[Japanese missions to Sui China|Third embassy to Sui China]].
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from [[Yōka Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miidani Kofun cluster | native_name =箕谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miidani Kofun Group 2go-fun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miidani Kofun-gun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miidani Kofun-gun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|24|1|N|134|45|13|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Yabu, Hyōgo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|1|N|134|45|13|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miidani Kofun cluster'''|箕谷古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yokachō Koyama neighborhood of the city of [[Yabu, Hyōgo]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The group consists of five tumuli, four of which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139097 |title=箕谷古墳群 |trans-title= Miidani Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miidani Kofun cluster is located in the center of the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]], on a hill on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Yagi River, at an elevation of 83 to 93 meters. The group consists of four small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style (circular) Kofuns, all of which have been considerably flattened by erosion, exposing the horizontal stone [[burial chamber]]s. The No.2 tumulus was slightly oval, measuring 12 by 14 meters; the No.3 tumulus was even more oval at 13.5 by 9.5 meters, and had ''[[fukiishi]]''. The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.
From [[grave goods]] recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of [[Sue pottery]] pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the [[Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] revealed had a [[Chinese calendar]] dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that [[Ono no Imoko]] departed on the [[Japanese missions to Sui China|Third embassy to Sui China]].
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from [[Yōka Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Front of Miidani Kofun Group.jpg|Panorama Miidani2goufun-20080923.JPG|Kofun No.2 Miidani Kofun Group 3go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.3 Miidani Kofun Group 4go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.4 Miidani Kofun Group 5go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.5 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Miidani Kofun Group}} *[https://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/midanikohun Yabu Tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/shakaikyoiku/1/4/mi123/index.html Yabu City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miidani Kofun cluster | native_name =箕谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miidani Kofun Group 2go-fun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miidani Kofun-gun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miidani Kofun-gun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|24|1|N|134|45|13|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Yabu, Hyōgo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|1|N|134|45|13|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miidani Kofun cluster'''|箕谷古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yokachō Koyama neighborhood of the city of [[Yabu, Hyōgo]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The group consists of five tumuli, four of which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139097 |title=箕谷古墳群 |trans-title= Miidani Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miidani Kofun cluster is located in the center of the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]], on a hill on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Yagi River, at an elevation of 83 to 93 meters. The group consists of four small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style (circular) Kofuns, all of which have been considerably flattened by erosion, exposing the horizontal stone [[burial chamber]]s. The No.2 tumulus was slightly oval, measuring 12 by 14 meters; the No.3 tumulus was even more oval at 13.5 by 9.5 meters, and had ''[[fukiishi]]''. The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.
From [[grave goods]] recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of [[Sue pottery]] pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the [[Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] revealed had a [[Chinese calendar]] dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that [[Ono no Imoko]] departed on the [[Japanese missions to Sui China|Third embassy to Sui China]].
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from [[Yōka Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Front of Miidani Kofun Group.jpg|Panorama Miidani2goufun-20080923.JPG|Kofun No.2 Miidani Kofun Group 3go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.3 Miidani Kofun Group 4go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.4 Miidani Kofun Group 5go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.5 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The group consists of four small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style (circular) Kofuns, all of which have been considerably flattened by erosion, exposing the horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. The No.2 tumulus was slightly oval, measuring 12 by 14 meters; the No.3 tumulus was even more oval at 13.5 by 9.5 meters, and had ''[[fukiishi]]''. The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.
From [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of [[Sue pottery]] pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the [[:en:Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] revealed had a [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]] dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that [[Ono no Imoko]] departed on the [[:en:Japanese missions to Sui China|Third embassy to Sui China]].
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from [[Yōka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miidani Kofun cluster | native_name =箕谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miidani Kofun Group 2go-fun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miidani Kofun-gun | map_type = Japan Hyōgo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miidani Kofun-gun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|24|1|N|134|45|13|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Yabu, Hyōgo|Yabu, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|1|N|134|45|13|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miidani Kofun cluster'''|箕谷古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yokachō Koyama neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Yabu, Hyōgo|Yabu, Hyōgo]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The group consists of five tumuli, four of which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139097 |title=箕谷古墳群 |trans-title= Miidani Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miidani Kofun cluster is located in the center of the [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima region]], on a hill on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Yagi River, at an elevation of 83 to 93 meters. The group consists of four small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style (circular) Kofuns, all of which have been considerably flattened by erosion, exposing the horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. The No.2 tumulus was slightly oval, measuring 12 by 14 meters; the No.3 tumulus was even more oval at 13.5 by 9.5 meters, and had ''[[fukiishi]]''. The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.
From [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of [[Sue pottery]] pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the [[:en:Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] revealed had a [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]] dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] departed on the [[:en:Japanese missions to Sui China|Third embassy to Sui China]].
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from [[Yōka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Front of Miidani Kofun Group.jpg|Panorama Miidani2goufun-20080923.JPG|Kofun No.2 Miidani Kofun Group 3go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.3 Miidani Kofun Group 4go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.4 Miidani Kofun Group 5go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.5 </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]

From [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the [[:en:Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] revealed had a [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]] dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] departed on the [[:en:Japanese missions to Sui China|Third embassy to Sui China]].
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The group consists of five tumuli, four of which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139097 |title=箕谷古墳群 |trans-title= Miidani Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>

The group consists of four small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style (circular) Kofuns, all of which have been considerably flattened by erosion, exposing the horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the [[:en:Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] revealed had a [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]] dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] departed on the [[:en:Japanese missions to Sui China|Third embassy to Sui China]].
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Miidani Kofun cluster'''|箕谷古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yokachō Koyama neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Yabu, Hyōgo|Yabu, Hyōgo]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The group consists of five tumuli, four of which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139097 |title=箕谷古墳群 |trans-title= Miidani Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miidani Kofun cluster is located in the center of the [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima region]], on a hill on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Yagi River, at an elevation of 83 to 93 meters. The group consists of four small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style (circular) Kofuns, all of which have been considerably flattened by erosion, exposing the horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. The No.2 tumulus was slightly oval, measuring 12 by 14 meters; the No.3 tumulus was even more oval at 13.5 by 9.5 meters, and had ''[[fukiishi]]''. The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.
From [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the [[:en:Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] revealed had a [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]] dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] departed on the [[:en:Japanese missions to Sui China|Third embassy to Sui China]].
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Miidani Kofun cluster'''|箕谷古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yokachō Koyama neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Yabu, Hyōgo|Yabu, Hyōgo]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The group consists of five tumuli, four of which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139097 |title=箕谷古墳群 |trans-title= Miidani Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miidani Kofun cluster is located in the center of the [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima region]], on a hill on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Yagi River, at an elevation of 83 to 93 meters. The group consists of four small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style (circular) Kofuns, all of which have been considerably flattened by erosion, exposing the horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. The No.2 tumulus was slightly oval, measuring 12 by 14 meters; the No.3 tumulus was even more oval at 13.5 by 9.5 meters, and had ''[[fukiishi]]''. The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.
From [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the [[:en:Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] revealed had a [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]] dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] departed on the [[:en:Japanese missions to Sui China|Third embassy to Sui China]].
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Miidani Kofun cluster'''|箕谷古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yokachō Koyama neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Yabu, Hyōgo|Yabu, Hyōgo]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The group consists of five tumuli, four of which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139097 |title=箕谷古墳群 |trans-title= Miidani Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miidani Kofun cluster is located in the center of the [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima region]], on a hill on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Yagi River, at an elevation of 83 to 93 meters. The group consists of four small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style (circular) Kofuns, all of which have been considerably flattened by erosion, exposing the horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. The No.2 tumulus was slightly oval, measuring 12 by 14 meters; the No.3 tumulus was even more oval at 13.5 by 9.5 meters, and had ''[[fukiishi]]''. The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.
From [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the [[:en:Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] revealed had a [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]] dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] departed on the [[:en:Japanese missions to Sui China|Third embassy to Sui China]].
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The No.2 tumulus was slightly oval, measuring 12 by 14 meters; the No.3 tumulus was even more oval at 13.5 by 9.5 meters, and had ''[[fukiishi]]''. The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.
From [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the [[:en:Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] revealed had a [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]] dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] departed on the [[:en:Japanese missions to Sui China|Third embassy to Sui China]].
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Front of Miidani Kofun Group.jpg|Panorama Miidani2goufun-20080923.JPG|Kofun No.2 Miidani Kofun Group 3go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.3 Miidani Kofun Group 4go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.4 Miidani Kofun Group 5go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.5 </gallery>

==Overview== The Miidani Kofun cluster is located in the center of the [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima region]], on a hill on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Yagi River, at an elevation of 83 to 93 meters. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the [[:en:Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] revealed had a [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]] dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] departed on the [[:en:Japanese missions to Sui China|Third embassy to Sui China]].

The No.2 tumulus was slightly oval, measuring 12 by 14 meters; the No.3 tumulus was even more oval at 13.5 by 9.5 meters, and had ''[[fukiishi]]''. The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.
From [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the [[:en:Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] revealed had a [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]] dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] departed on the [[:en:Japanese missions to Sui China|Third embassy to Sui China]].
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The group consists of five tumuli, four of which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139097 |title=箕谷古墳群 |trans-title= Miidani Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miidani Kofun cluster is located in the center of the [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima region]], on a hill on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Yagi River, at an elevation of 83 to 93 meters. The group consists of four small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style (circular) Kofuns, all of which have been considerably flattened by erosion, exposing the horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. The No.2 tumulus was slightly oval, measuring 12 by 14 meters; the No.3 tumulus was even more oval at 13.5 by 9.5 meters, and had ''[[fukiishi]]''. The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.
From [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the [[:en:Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] revealed had a [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]] dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] departed on the [[:en:Japanese missions to Sui China|Third embassy to Sui China]].
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Front of Miidani Kofun Group.jpg|Panorama Miidani2goufun-20080923.JPG|Kofun No.2 Miidani Kofun Group 3go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.3 Miidani Kofun Group 4go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.4 Miidani Kofun Group 5go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.5 </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Miidani Kofun cluster'''|箕谷古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yokachō Koyama neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Yabu, Hyōgo|Yabu, Hyōgo]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The group consists of five tumuli, four of which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139097 |title=箕谷古墳群 |trans-title= Miidani Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miidani Kofun cluster is located in the center of the [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima region]], on a hill on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Yagi River, at an elevation of 83 to 93 meters. The group consists of four small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style (circular) Kofuns, all of which have been considerably flattened by erosion, exposing the horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. The No.2 tumulus was slightly oval, measuring 12 by 14 meters; the No.3 tumulus was even more oval at 13.5 by 9.5 meters, and had ''[[fukiishi]]''. The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.
From [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the [[:en:Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] revealed had a [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]] dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] departed on the [[:en:Japanese missions to Sui China|Third embassy to Sui China]].
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Front of Miidani Kofun Group.jpg|Panorama Miidani2goufun-20080923.JPG|Kofun No.2 Miidani Kofun Group 3go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.3 Miidani Kofun Group 4go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.4 Miidani Kofun Group 5go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.5 </gallery>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Miidani Kofun Group]][[en:Miidani Kofun Cluster]][[ja:箕谷古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Miidani Kofun Group]][[en:Miidani Kofun Cluster]][[ja:箕谷古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miidani Kofun Group]][[en:Miidani Kofun Cluster]][[ja:箕谷古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11603145]] Wikidata name: Miidani Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miidani Kofun cluster | native_name =箕谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miidani Kofun Group 2go-fun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miidani Kofun-gun | map_type = Japan Hyōgo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miidani Kofun-gun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|24|1|N|134|45|13|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}}; {{Coord|35.400277777778|N|134.75361111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Yabu, Hyōgo|en|Yabu, Hyōgo|WD=Q869014|lt=Yabu, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}; {{ill|Yabu|WD=Q869014}}| region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|1|N|134|45|13|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miidani Kofun cluster'''|箕谷古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yokachō Koyama neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Yabu, Hyōgo|en|Yabu, Hyōgo|WD=Q869014|lt=Yabu, Hyōgo}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The group consists of five tumuli, four of which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139097 |title=箕谷古墳群 |trans-title= Miidani Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miidani Kofun cluster is located in the center of the {{ill|Tajima Province|en|Tajima Province|WD=Q1053727|lt=Tajima region}}, on a hill on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Yagi River, at an elevation of 83 to 93 meters. The group consists of four small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style (circular) Kofuns, all of which have been considerably flattened by erosion, exposing the horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. The No.2 tumulus was slightly oval, measuring 12 by 14 meters; the No.3 tumulus was even more oval at 13.5 by 9.5 meters, and had ''[[fukiishi]]''. The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.
From {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the {{ill|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|en|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|WD=Q3297691|lt=Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties}} revealed had a {{ill|Chinese calendar|en|Chinese calendar|WD=Q134032|lt=Chinese calendar}} dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that {{ill|Ono no Imoko|en|Ono no Imoko|WD=Q1284072|lt=Ono no Imoko}} departed on the {{ill|Japanese missions to Sui China|en|Japanese missions to Sui China|WD=Q15401774|lt=Third embassy to Sui China}}.
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka Station|en|Yōka Station|WD=Q870988|lt=Yōka Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Front of Miidani Kofun Group.jpg|Panorama Miidani2goufun-20080923.JPG|Kofun No.2 Miidani Kofun Group 3go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.3 Miidani Kofun Group 4go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.4 Miidani Kofun Group 5go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.5 </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|WD=Q104629006|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Miidani Kofun Group}} *[https://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/midanikohun Yabu Tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/shakaikyoiku/1/4/mi123/index.html Yabu City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.

The {{nihongo|'''Miidani Kofun cluster'''|箕谷古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yokachō Koyama neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Yabu, Hyōgo|en|Yabu, Hyōgo|WD=Q869014|lt=Yabu, Hyōgo}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The group consists of five tumuli, four of which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139097 |title=箕谷古墳群 |trans-title= Miidani Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miidani Kofun cluster is located in the center of the {{ill|Tajima Province|en|Tajima Province|WD=Q1053727|lt=Tajima region}}, on a hill on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Yagi River, at an elevation of 83 to 93 meters. The group consists of four small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style (circular) Kofuns, all of which have been considerably flattened by erosion, exposing the horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. The No.2 tumulus was slightly oval, measuring 12 by 14 meters; the No.3 tumulus was even more oval at 13.5 by 9.5 meters, and had ''[[fukiishi]]''. The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.
From {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the {{ill|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|en|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|WD=Q3297691|lt=Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties}} revealed had a {{ill|Chinese calendar|en|Chinese calendar|WD=Q134032|lt=Chinese calendar}} dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that {{ill|Ono no Imoko|en|Ono no Imoko|WD=Q1284072|lt=Ono no Imoko}} departed on the {{ill|Japanese missions to Sui China|en|Japanese missions to Sui China|WD=Q15401774|lt=Third embassy to Sui China}}.
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka Station|en|Yōka Station|WD=Q870988|lt=Yōka Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Front of Miidani Kofun Group.jpg|Panorama Miidani2goufun-20080923.JPG|Kofun No.2 Miidani Kofun Group 3go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.3 Miidani Kofun Group 4go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.4 Miidani Kofun Group 5go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.5 </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Miidani Kofun Group]][[en:Miidani Kofun Cluster]][[ja:箕谷古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Miidani Kofun Group]][[en:Miidani Kofun Cluster]][[ja:箕谷古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miidani Kofun Group]][[en:Miidani Kofun Cluster]][[ja:箕谷古墳群]]
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miidani Kofun cluster | native_name =箕谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miidani Kofun Group 2go-fun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miidani Kofun-gun | map_type = Japan Hyōgo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miidani Kofun-gun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|24|1|N|134|45|13|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}}; {{Coord|35.400277777778|N|134.75361111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Yabu, Hyōgo|en|Yabu, Hyōgo|WD=Q869014|lt=Yabu, Hyōgo|1=Yabu, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Yabu|WD=Q869014|1=Yabu, Hyōgo}}| region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|1|N|134|45|13|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miidani Kofun cluster'''|箕谷古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yokachō Koyama neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Yabu, Hyōgo|en|Yabu, Hyōgo|WD=Q869014|lt=Yabu, Hyōgo|1=Yabu, Hyōgo}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The group consists of five tumuli, four of which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139097 |title=箕谷古墳群 |trans-title= Miidani Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miidani Kofun cluster is located in the center of the {{ill|Tajima Province|en|Tajima Province|WD=Q1053727|lt=Tajima region|1=Tajima Province}}, on a hill on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Yagi River, at an elevation of 83 to 93 meters. The group consists of four small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style (circular) Kofuns, all of which have been considerably flattened by erosion, exposing the horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. The No.2 tumulus was slightly oval, measuring 12 by 14 meters; the No.3 tumulus was even more oval at 13.5 by 9.5 meters, and had ''[[fukiishi]]''. The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.
From {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the {{ill|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|en|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|WD=Q3297691|lt=Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|1=Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties}} revealed had a {{ill|Chinese calendar|en|Chinese calendar|WD=Q134032|lt=Chinese calendar|1=Chinese calendar}} dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that {{ill|Ono no Imoko|en|Ono no Imoko|WD=Q1284072|lt=Ono no Imoko|1=Ono no Imoko}} departed on the {{ill|Japanese missions to Sui China|en|Japanese missions to Sui China|WD=Q15401774|lt=Third embassy to Sui China|1=Japanese missions to Sui China}}.
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka Station|en|Yōka Station|WD=Q870988|lt=Yōka Station|1=Yōka Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Front of Miidani Kofun Group.jpg|Panorama Miidani2goufun-20080923.JPG|Kofun No.2 Miidani Kofun Group 3go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.3 Miidani Kofun Group 4go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.4 Miidani Kofun Group 5go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.5 </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|WD=Q104629006|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miidani Kofun cluster | native_name =箕谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miidani Kofun Group 2go-fun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miidani Kofun-gun | map_type = Japan Hyōgo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miidani Kofun-gun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|24|1|N|134|45|13|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}}; {{Coord|35.400277777778|N|134.75361111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Yabu, Hyōgo|en|Yabu, Hyōgo|WD=Q869014|lt=Yabu, Hyōgo|1=Yabu, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Yabu|WD=Q869014|1=Yabu, Hyōgo}}| region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|1|N|134|45|13|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miidani Kofun cluster'''|箕谷古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yokachō Koyama neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Yabu, Hyōgo|en|Yabu, Hyōgo|WD=Q869014|lt=Yabu, Hyōgo|1=Yabu, Hyōgo}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The group consists of five tumuli, four of which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139097 |title=箕谷古墳群 |trans-title= Miidani Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miidani Kofun cluster is located in the center of the {{ill|Tajima Province|en|Tajima Province|WD=Q1053727|lt=Tajima region|1=Tajima Province}}, on a hill on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Yagi River, at an elevation of 83 to 93 meters. The group consists of four small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style (circular) Kofuns, all of which have been considerably flattened by erosion, exposing the horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. The No.2 tumulus was slightly oval, measuring 12 by 14 meters; the No.3 tumulus was even more oval at 13.5 by 9.5 meters, and had ''[[fukiishi]]''. The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.
From {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the {{ill|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|en|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|WD=Q3297691|lt=Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|1=Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties}} revealed had a {{ill|Chinese calendar|en|Chinese calendar|WD=Q134032|lt=Chinese calendar|1=Chinese calendar}} dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that {{ill|Ono no Imoko|en|Ono no Imoko|WD=Q1284072|lt=Ono no Imoko|1=Ono no Imoko}} departed on the {{ill|Japanese missions to Sui China|en|Japanese missions to Sui China|WD=Q15401774|lt=Third embassy to Sui China|1=Japanese missions to Sui China}}.
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka Station|en|Yōka Station|WD=Q870988|lt=Yōka Station|1=Yōka Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Front of Miidani Kofun Group.jpg|Panorama Miidani2goufun-20080923.JPG|Kofun No.2 Miidani Kofun Group 3go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.3 Miidani Kofun Group 4go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.4 Miidani Kofun Group 5go-fun.jpg|Kofun No.5 </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|WD=Q104629006|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Miidani Kofun Group}} *[https://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/midanikohun Yabu Tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/shakaikyoiku/1/4/mi123/index.html Yabu City home page] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

The {{nihongo|'''Miidani Kofun cluster'''|箕谷古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yokachō Koyama neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Yabu, Hyōgo|en|Yabu, Hyōgo|qid=Q869014|lt=Yabu, Hyōgo|1=Yabu, Hyōgo}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The group consists of five tumuli, four of which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139097 |title=箕谷古墳群 |trans-title= Miidani Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miidani Kofun cluster is located in the center of the {{ill|Tajima Province|en|Tajima Province|qid=Q1053727|lt=Tajima region|1=Tajima Province}}, on a hill on the left bank of the lower reaches of the Yagi River, at an elevation of 83 to 93 meters. The group consists of four small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style (circular) Kofuns, all of which have been considerably flattened by erosion, exposing the horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. The No.2 tumulus was slightly oval, measuring 12 by 14 meters; the No.3 tumulus was even more oval at 13.5 by 9.5 meters, and had ''[[fukiishi]]''. The No.4 tumulus and No.5 tumulus were more circular, with a diameters of seven meters and six meters respectively.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The No.1 tumulus is located further to the west, and is not part of the National Historic Site designation.
From {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} recovered during an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted in 1983, it is estimated that the No.2 tumulus was built from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, the No.3 tumulus and No.4 tumulus in the middle of the 7th century, and the No.5 tumulus in the latter half of the 7th century. Some 103 items were recovered, including many examples of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery, iron rings, iron horse harnesses, and iron swords. Of especial note was an iron sword recovered from the No.2 tumulus, which an X-ray analysis at the {{ill|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|en|Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|qid=Q3297691|lt=Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties|1=Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties}} revealed had a {{ill|Chinese calendar|en|Chinese calendar|qid=Q134032|lt=Chinese calendar|1=Chinese calendar}} dated copper-inlay inscription corresponding to 608 AD.<ref name="Shiseki"/> This is the same year that {{ill|Ono no Imoko|en|Ono no Imoko|qid=Q1284072|lt=Ono no Imoko|1=Ono no Imoko}} departed on the {{ill|Japanese missions to Sui China|en|Japanese missions to Sui China|qid=Q15401774|lt=Third embassy to Sui China|1=Japanese missions to Sui China}}.
The site is now a public park, and is located about 13 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka Station|en|Yōka Station|qid=Q870988|lt=Yōka Station|1=Yōka Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|qid=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
